MV. UNIVERSE KAISA

BLT  2004 IMABARI /JAPAN

DWT 28,388 MT ON 9.778 MTRS (SUMMER)

LOA 169.26 / BEAM 27.2

GRT 16,954 / NRT 10,134

5 HO / 5 HO

GRAIN:37,523 CBM (1,325,110 CBF) / BALE:35,762 CBM (1,262,937 CBF)

CRANES: 4 X 30.5 MTONS

SPEED/CONSUMPTION

BALLAST: ABT 12.5 KNOTS ON ABT 17.0 MT IFO (380CST) + ABT 0.2 MT MGO

LADEN:    ABT 12.0 KNOTS ON ABT 18.0 MT IFO (380CST) + ABT 0.2 MT MGO

IN PORT IDLE: ABT 2.2 MTONS IFO + 0.3 MTONS MGO

IN PORT WORKING: ABT 3.5 MTONS IFO + 1.2 MTONS MGO

SPEED AND CONSUMPTION IS ON BASIS OF THE CONDITION OF MAXIMUM BEAUFORT WIND
FORCE 4 AND DOUGLAS SEA STATE 3 AND NO ADVERSE CURRENT.

AN UNDERPERFORMANCE CLAIM CAN ONLY BE SUBMITTED IN RESPECT OF PERIODS DURING
WHICH THE VESSEL ENCOUNTERS WEATHER UPTO BEAUFORT 4 AND/OR DOUGLAS SEASTATE
3 (SIGNIFICANT WAVE UP TO 1.25 M MAX N SWELL WAVE UP TO 2 M MAX)  AND NO
ADVERSE CURRENT AND NO NEGATIVE INFLUENCE OF SWELL

SPEED "ABOUT" MEANS 0.5 KNOTS DOWNWARDS AND BUNKER "ABOUT" MEANS 5%, UPWARDS
IN CALCULATING OVER-CONSUMPTION BUT NO ALLOWANCE IN FUEL
UNDER-CONSUMPTION.ALL SPEED AND CONSUMPTION FIGURES ARE AVERAGE FIGURES
DURING C/P PERIOD

VESSELS MAIN ENGINE MAY BURN MDO/MGO AND/OR EXTRA IFO IN CASE OF

EMERGENCY OR HILE NAVIGATING/MANEUVERING IN NARROW/SHALLOW/RESTRICTED

WATERS, CANALS, RIVERS, ENTERING/LEAVING PORTS/CANALS, STARTING/STOPPING

THE ENGINE, BALLAST WATER EXCHANGING, CLEANING VESSEL$B!G(BS HOLDS,

BALLASTING/DEBALLASTING OPERATION, OR BAD/COLD WEATHER CONDITION ETC.

ALWAYS AT THE DISCRETION OF THE MASTER WITH REASONABLE EXCUSE.

ADA
